Additional documents may be requested as needed. In India, Medical Linear Accelerators receive market clearance through the AERB (Atomic Energy Review Board).  Accelerators are voregistered as medical devices in India. Overview of India clearance process via AERB: • First step is the NOC “No Objection Certificate” - this bears the name of a hospital and configuration information for only one model (other NOCs are required for other configurations or models) • Install this one model per the NOC and the AERB will test it, or observe testing of it to obtain data. • After testing is approved, then a TAC (Type Approval Certificate) certificated is issued, for that one configuration. title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks
text_content: Compliance is checked as follows: a) SITE TEST grade B – Procedure: at one NOMINAL ENERGY for each RADIATION TYPE, verify the transition to the TERMINATION OF IRRADIATION state. Subclause 201.10.101.5.2 a does not apply, because the operating parameters cannot be changed during INTERRUPTION OF IRRADIATION. The system provides a means to select and run treatment plans only using the treatment software in the control room. No interface exists for changing operating parameters in the treatment software. Even in the case of recalibration performed after interrupting treatment, a machine performance check (MPC) is run to check that operational parameters are within specification. b) SITE TEST grade B – Procedure: at one NOMINAL ENERGY for each RADIATION TYPE, verify the restart of IRRADIATION after INTERRUPTION OF IRRADIATION. Methods IEC 60601-2-1 Edition 4.0 2020 Test Report for 201.10.101.5.2 – INTERRUPTION OF IRRADIATION

source: D65887 HAL4.0 IEC 60601-2-1 (2020) 201.10.101.1.1.5 - TERMINATION OF IRRADIATION by DOSE MONITORING SYSTEM Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks
text_content: Compliance is checked as follows: a) b) c) e) TYPE TEST grade A – Statement regarding the means used to ensure compliance. Ref a) and c) - Grade A Type Tests – Design Statement The ability of the secondary channel, that is the BGM FPGA level, to terminate irradiation is verified between treatments as part of the dosimetry test during the mode (radiation type and energy) selection process which is called the CalCheck cycle. The CalCheck cycle includes a first check that the continuity of the cables for all ion chamber plate signals is verified. This continuity check is achieved by introducing a step change in the ion chamber power supply voltage level and then detecting the corresponding induced charges in the ion chamber collection plates. This is needed because later the dosimetry check is performed locally on the BGM-CONT only. Such a test is valid because passing the continuity test can prove that the cable connection and the connections through the ion chamber, are all intact.

source: D65924 HAL4.0 IEC 60601-2-1 (2020) 201.10.101.1.9 - VERIFICATION and DISPLAY of MULTI-LEAF COLLIMATOR shapes - IONIZING RADIATION during BEAM HOLD Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks
text_content: Compliance is checked as follows: b) to e) TYPE TEST grade A – Statement regarding the means used to ensure compliance. Ref b) Grade A Type Test The MLC display functions as a real-time display during irradiation for the actual position of the MLC leaves. The design of the display is such that both banks, proximal and distal, are overlapped in a dual-layer arrangement. The MLC display shows only the actual position during irradiation to effectively communicate to users where the MLC leaves are positioned throughout irradiation. If any MLC leaf position deviates from its intended position during irradiation, the system will raise a fault with beam impact. Additionally, if an MLC leaf deviates from the planned position, the deviating MLC leaf on the MLC display will embody a yellow color for deviations that are within the warning tolerance, and a red color for deviations that exceed the fault tolerance.
